title: Gastrochilus somae
intro: Gastrochilus somae, also known as the Soma's lip orchid, is a species of terrestrial orchid native to the Himalayas. This plant is valued for its medicinal properties, which include anti-inflammatory, antibacterial, and antifungal activities, providing relief from various health issues such as fever, digestive problems, and skin infections. The bioactive constituents of Gastrochilus somae include flavonoids, phenolic acids, and terpenoids, which contribute to its medicinal properties. In traditional Ayurvedic medicine, Gastrochilus somae is commonly used to prepare herbal remedies, including teas, infusions, and decoctions, which are used to treat a range of health conditions.

The classification of Gastrochilus somae is further divided into the genus Gastrochilus, a group of orchids characterized by their relatively small size and delicate appearance.
